### Post by lehjr on 2012-09-09
I haven't tried the script yet, but this is what I get in Mint 13 using **locate aufs.ko**

```
/lib/modules/3.2.0-23-generic/kernel/ubuntu/aufs/aufs.ko
/lib/modules/3.2.0-27-generic/kernel/ubuntu/aufs/aufs.ko
/lib/modules/3.2.0-29-generic/kernel/ubuntu/aufs/aufs.ko
/lib/modules/3.2.0-30-generic/kernel/ubuntu/aufs/aufs.ko
```

I'll check my QQ installation in a minute and see what that has.
Edit:
Checked and apparently QQ has aufs support in the kernel source, but not the modules weren't built. 

```
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13/ubuntu/aufs
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13/ubuntu/aufs/Kconfig
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13/ubuntu/aufs/Makefile
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/bdev
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/br
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/branch
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/fs.h
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/poll.h
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/sbilist.h
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/bdev/loop.h
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/br/fuse.h
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/br/hfsplus.h
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/br/ramfs.h
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/branch/max
/usr/src/linux-headers-3.5.0-13-generic/include/config/aufs/branch/max/127.h

```

I'll try the script in a few minutes and let you know how it goes. If it doesn't work, you may have to build the kernel from source, something we rarely need to do these days.

Edit:
Had to comb the repo for the last script that uses aufs (614). So far, it seems to be mounting the image, nothing beyond that, but at least aufs is working.

On the other hand, I did try the most recent version of the script and it works well on my machine, even if it is a bit slow, a bit faster when using LXDE. I have a really old machine though, and I imagine that most modern machines will have little trouble running this. Either way,  once again, nice work. I just wish the Ubuntu devs would get involved and help get Wayland moving along instead of waiting for everyone else to do the work.

### Post by lehjr on 2012-09-09
Looks like that would be where third party drivers are added and then they have to be added to the config and make file. Notice that in the Makefile in the same directory, the line 
```
#obj-$(CONFIG_AUFS_FS)		+= aufs/
```
is commented out with a "#". So it's likely that it would get ignored at build time. But to answer your question, yes, that's where it would come from.

I was looking for a way to build the kernel module without having to build the whole kernel, but so far I haven't found much information on this one.

---

### Post by lehjr on 2012-09-09
Interesting, especially comment #17 [https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux/+bug/943119](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux/+bug/943119)

Edit: 
see also [http://ubuntu.5.n6.nabble.com/RFC-AUFS-disabled-for-12-04-td4532153.html](http://ubuntu.5.n6.nabble.com/RFC-AUFS-disabled-for-12-04-td4532153.html)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2012-10-28
HI.

I updated the CD for Wayland 1.0. I had to work around the fact that the software rendering doesn't work, by using glesv2 instead of the full GL for weston clients. This causes the gears, and the screensaver to be disabled.

Also xwayland, QT, and SDL still have to be ported.

But you can try it out here:

[http://sourceforge.net/projects/rebeccablackos/files/WithWayland1.0/RebeccaBlackLinux.iso/download](http://sourceforge.net/projects/rebeccablackos/files/WithWayland1.0/RebeccaBlackLinux.iso/download)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2012-11-12
> **cecilpierce said:**
> The 3.5.0-18 kernel freezes with xwayland, I read some where that the 3.7.0-0 works with nouveau and nVidia, installed it and it works great! Only uses 262k memory verses 790k.
About the only thing that I could not get to work was Weston Gears.

Is it posible to run wayland from recovery mode in a tty ?

P.S. what happen to the screen shot app?

you can try running 
```
/opt/bin/weston-launch
```you might need to run 
```
mkdir /run/user/root/ 
```first

I think the screenshot app doesn't get installed anymore, as it was a recommends dependency of a package, and I only install required dependencies to reduce the ISO size... I could install it if you want, but you can take a screenshot of Wayland by pressing super+s

EDIT: weston-gears doesn't work because of the mesa issues... I had to compile weston with a different option as a workaround, to not use full GL, and as a result, weston-gears doesn't compile, along with the screensaver.

Yes. ```
sudo update-rbos 
``` updates all the files from the SVN.


If you run
```
sudo update-rbos build
```
it builds everything as well.

What happens to synaptic?

are you trying to run synaptic off of a weston-terminal, or an xterm? If you run it under a weston-terminal, it has wayland vars, which will make Synaptic use the GTK under Wayland. Some GTK apps don't work correctly under Wayland.

### Post by cecilpierce on 2012-11-29

### Post by nerdopolis on 2012-11-29
> **cecilpierce said:**
> From grub I use /boot/vmlinuz root=/dev/sdb7 ro single to go to tty1 and then type westoncaller but it will only run with sudo.

Why not as user instead of root ?
you would probably want to run ```
/opt/bin/weston-launch
``` if you are running it on bare hardware like that. It needs permissions. /opt/bin/weston-launch is setuid.

### Post by cecilpierce on 2012-12-25

### Post by nerdopolis on 2012-12-25
> **cecilpierce said:**
> Haven't seen much in awhile, wondering how its going?

I think I just fixed a DNS issue with the CD.

As far as Wayland goes, I changed the way I built QT so that now the QT applications don't needlessly connect to X when using qtwayland, and also changed the way I built it so now I get test web browsers (minimal, but native wayland web browsers!) I also added more GTK to the CD, as lots work.


I will probably release a new ISO once I start building kde-baseapps (and possibly kde-workspace) against kdelibs frameworks to get real native KDE wayland apps. Hopefully after I build KDE, I can get a native Wayland application launcher working, which will therefore have Wayland environment variables, to run Wayland apps easier.

(the problem is the current application launcher is an X app, and it has to have the Wayland variables unset)

---

As far as Wayland goes, I changed the way I built QT so that now the QT applications don't needlessly connect to X when using qtwayland, and also changed the way I built it so now I get test web browsers (minimal, but native wayland web browsers!) I also added more GTK to the CD, as lots work.


I will probably release a new ISO once I start building kde-baseapps (and possibly kde-workspace) against kdelibs frameworks to get real native KDE wayland apps. Hopefully after I build KDE, I can get a native Wayland application launcher working, which will therefore have Wayland environment variables, to run Wayland apps easier.

(the problem is the current application launcher is an X app, and it has to have the Wayland variables unset)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-01-08
> **cecilpierce said:**
> Hey nerdopolis, hows it going? Any news on the new iso, my old one needs a work over...:p


The new ISOs should be on.


I applied a recently created patch from "Stskeeps" to Mesa, which fixes issues with lots of Wayland clients that couldn't run on some cards.

I also added the option on the logon dialog to use weston with pure software rendering, as described in the Phoronix article.

This Live CD also has the 3 simple kdelibs test apps working again.


Unfortunately, the QT applications titlebars are not as funtional as the last ISO. The branch that had the interactive features for QT titlebars lost compatibility with QT changes.

---

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-01-14
> **lehjr said:**
> nerdopolis, how is it building RBOS now vs a few months ago? Has anything gotten better (or worse) with with Wayland, or Qt, or even GTK?


Things have gotten better. Much better. Now that Wayland is a stable protocol everything is more likely to compile.

And as far as building my Live CD goes, I overhauled the build scripts on the last week of 2012 so that I do not have to recompile everything EVERY ISO build, cutting hours of build time down to 20 minutes.

---

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-01-14
> **cecilpierce said:**
> Well I DL it again and the checksum was right but can't get the flash drive to boot, tried unetbootin, kde's startup disk creator and dd, get errors.
I'm going to buy some DVD's tomarrow and see If they will work.

p.s. just looked and the jan 7 is missing now ???


January 7th is replaced with January 12th. 

DD won't work. I disable that feature from Remastersys currently with my scripts, as 
isohybrid says that the sector size is off, and some BIOSes won't like it.

But that is interesting that the kde startup disk creator won't work for you, as I use it all the time with my distro to test it out on my spare test laptop.

> **nerdopolis said:**
> Things have gotten better. Much better. Now that Wayland is a stable protocol everything is more likely to compile.

And as far as building my Live CD goes, I overhauled the build scripts on the last week of 2012 so that I do not have to recompile everything EVERY ISO build, cutting hours of build time down to 20 minutes.

That's great!! Since the CD is KDE based, which uses Qt, do you use GTK at all? Also, any thoughts on Green Island? 

Anyway, after doing a bit of reading recently, I have once again concluded that that your CD will probably be the closest thing to an official Ubuntu/Wayland incarnation for the foreseeable future, since Ubuntu is currently dependent on Compiz for Unity, and the Compiz developer Sam Spilsbury left, not that he had any intentions of porting Compiz to Wayland anyway. I know they don't want to enable the Wayland backend for GTK because they don't want to have unused dependencies, but why not at least have an alternate GTK package with the Wayland backend enabled?

---

### Post by lehjr on 2013-02-10
> **cecilpierce said:**
> Yep, kinda looks like wayland will be on the back burner unless some one gets on the stick..:p

From what I can piece together, either Canonical will be using Wayland with their own compositor, or they'll be using some type of Android display server fork. I don't really see any alternative, but I do see points in both cases that only strengthen the need for this very project that nerdopolis is working on. 

_If Canonical decides to go with their own Wayland compositor:_
Since Wayland has largely been ignored by Cannonical, they'll need to see what works and doesn't work on their own distribution and come up with solutions to things that don't work. RBOS already has a jump on this. 

_If Canonical decides to a fork of Android code:_
The RBOS project may be the only easy means of using Wayland on Ubuntu. This is more likely, since they already have already displayed interest and some level of experience in Android, not to mention Android is already on production devices, while Wayland isn't ready for that phase quite yet.

Either way, regardless of what direction Canonical goes in, the work on RBOS is still needed and greatly appreciated.

---

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-05-21
> **cecilpierce said:**
> Seems like the last update did something bad, when I try to login it just goes back to login screen ???


You mean you ran update-rbos ?
update-rbos pretty much downloads the latest files off the SVN. (unless you specify it to run the compile scripts with the build option, which takes hours)

Lately, I've been pushing many commits *mostly* with incremental changes to one file, /usr/bin/waylandloginmanager
I'm so close to getting the loginmanager to work. I'm just trying to tweak the autologin to work correctly (which I think I'm pretty close), and the switch user dialog hopefully should work soon.

Once that works, I'll push a new ISO. It will be the first ISO that will be a true Wayland LiveCD because it doesn't use X for the login manager.

Sorry about that. I tend to use the SVN as my backup, in case something goes wrong where I mess up a file as a fix, so it was unstable...
Hopefully I can release tomorrow.

Edit: I *think* this latest commit *should* be stable, unless I made a big mistake with a missing [FONT=lucida console]}[/FONT] or [FONT=lucida console]fi[/FONT] or [FONT=lucida console]done[/FONT]. You can *try* update-rbos again, but I am currently building an ISO now so I can actually test it.

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-05-24
Hi.

I released the new ISO here

[http://sourceforge.net/projects/rebeccablackos/files/May%2024th%202013/RebeccaBlackLinux_i386.iso/download](http://sourceforge.net/projects/rebeccablackos/files/May%2024th%202013/RebeccaBlackLinux_i386.iso/download)

What's special about this one is that it now has the loginmanager I assembled for Wayland with bash.

Although I had to switch back to the mainline Weston, because despite the other tree I was using on the previous ISO had some out of tree features, like the taskbar in the panel, that repository is now out of date.

I described the login manager, and some information about its security here: [http://lists.freedesktop.org/archives/wayland-devel/2013-May/009475.html](http://lists.freedesktop.org/archives/wayland-devel/2013-May/009475.html)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-05-26
Blender appears to be running as X on that screenshot.

In order to record video in Weston, you will have to press super+r to start, and then super+r to stop recording

and then you will have to click on the movie icon between the speaker and the lightbulb so that it converts captured video data into a .webm file in the root of your home directory?

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-05-26
Hi. Sorry for the later reply, but early this morning I found out what the issue is. When I switched out the login manager, I didn't think to check the PWD. Anyway, instead of the PWD being the home directory, the PWD was the root directory. I pushed the fix to the SVN to change directories before starting weston.

---

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-05-30
That would seem to be a driver issue with kernel mode setting? What card do you have?

BTW: the vga option is only needed if you don't have Kernel mode setting, AND your video card driver doesn't create fbdev by default.

The problem is right now, if your card uses kernel mode setting, it does not use fbdev... 
...but I added right now the ability to force the loginmanager to set weston to use fbdev with a forcefbdev option, but it's in the SVN.

---

### Post by cecilpierce on 2013-05-30
> **nerdopolis said:**
> That would seem to be a driver issue with kernel mode setting? What card do you have?

BTW: the vga option is only needed if you don't have Kernel mode setting, AND your video card driver doesn't create fbdev by default.

The problem is right now, if your card uses kernel mode setting, it does not use fbdev... 
...but I added right now the ability to force the loginmanager to set weston to use fbdev with a forcefbdev option, but it's in the SVN.

So what should I do, add fbdev to kernel  line or update rblos ???

Oh its a built in nvidia 6100 card.

---

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-05-30
You would have to add *forcefbdev* to the kernel boot line **and **update from the SVN, because after I released it, I added support for the manual option in the loginmanager (The loginmanager is what parses /proc/cmdline to see if forcefbdev is there, it's not a real kernel option)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-05-31
Those folders are the downloaded git repos and build locations of all the software my build scripts are for, the list of dev packages is downloaded from the SVN, and parsed to install.

To get waylandloginmanager as your default back change the contents of /etc/X11/default-display-manager 
to /usr/bin/waylandloginmanager
Make sure there's NO leading space on that line! (I learned that the hard way [http://rebeccablackos.svn.sourceforge.net/viewvc/rebeccablackos/rebeccablacklinux_files/usr/import/etc/X11/default-display-manager?revision=1305&view=markup](http://rebeccablackos.svn.sourceforge.net/viewvc/rebeccablackos/rebeccablacklinux_files/usr/import/etc/X11/default-display-manager?revision=1305&view=markup) )

(Yes It's not a X11 display manager, but I wanted to make it best compatible with the other init scripts that use that file...)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-06-01
Are you doing sudo weston-launch? The problem is that if you run weston-launch as root, it will start weston as root, which has the ini file for the loginmanager.

weston-launch does NOT require sudo


as for the icons not working as root, that's because for the login display, they are just helper scripts that echo text into the loginmanagers FIFO. The loginmanager has to be running, you can manually start it
sudo waylandloginmanager

happen / or the fans on the computer going mad / or taking me to a black screen.

This may have been due to me not Formatting the disc and loading the new version ontop
of the older version - so that I could retain my history on the terminals to save time.
As that did not appear to work - I have now done a complete re-install and format ..... with the
March version - where the record desktop works but there is no login.

If there is a way to upgrade this without losing everything - then I will try again ...... but at the moment
after spending a night doing a lot of installing and experimenting to try to get it running am now wondering
whether to go through the build ....

Then manually add each of the packages that are built to see if they are installing properly.

The one that did not appear to install - which resulted in the black screen was weston ...... so I
used 

aptitude install weston

maybe this was giving me a older version of weston ..... as it pulled it from the ubuntu repository.

The thing was it worked and showed the display - but again leaving me with no way to get the 
applications or the loging to work ....... I am probably doing something wrong - but will now wait for a new build
before doing any more installs as the May version seems to leave me with no way of recording what I
am doing .......

I am quite happy to go through it manually to see exactly what is happening - but if you know why weston
is not upgrading from your script - can you let me know what I can check to see if that is the case.

Would gdebi and the package work ok from the build directory - for weston .......

I think that going through each one and doing them manually and maybe doing them in the order that
your script does them ....... maybe I should look at the script to see what it does - so that I can understand
better what is happening.

Am trying very hard to get the update on the newer version - mainly to get the desktop record working.

But that works fine in the older build - what I am having problems with also is resizing the Qtbrowser - this is
a 8 core machine and the speed of the window resize once it is dragged to maximize slows down considerably
and also at times leaves the mouse icon as the corner select not allowing for completion of resize.

That then leaves only one option for me - to shut down the window using ctrl + alt + backspace then to restart
I have checked from the CD and the same problems exist - no record and window resizing slows down and can
see the repaints as it gets larger ...... 1920 x 1080 screen size here ..... not sure if that is why I am getting the problems or if its something else.

I hope you can make some use of what I have said ...... if there is a sequence you would like me to try or some
log files you would like to see at the end of any attempt I make - please feel free to ask me and I will try to
do this in a logical order so that you can get some results that may help you.

I am typing this in the Qt Demo Browser from the March install and it seems ok ...... I have noticed on any
other upgrade I have done that this browser will start to open then dies off half way through it opening up 
also running firefox after a upgrade would crash the wayland window.

I see there are 7000 downloads now ..... would be interesting to see if anyone else is experiencing what I am
experiencing ..... there must be others that are watching this thread that can add some comments to see if
there is anything consistent across different installs .....

Overall I think the March version is very good and is workable .... the later version though for me and my system is not
at this moment in time ..... so will try to upgrade manually on the March version and see if I can keep the record 
working and get it up to a stage where the internet browsers keep running smoothly too.

That is as much as I can say at the moment ..... will try to record in more detail this time though .... and save some log
files or display them here ..... sort of tired now - so a lot of this is just my experience from last nights try outs and may not make a lot of sense without going through the same things that I tried out. Each install and upgrade takes a fair time though ..... think I did 4 or 5 installs last night as I kept changing the user keith .... keith1 keith2 ..... got to keith5
before I called it a night though and did a fresh install of the old version.

If there is a better and cleaner way of doing this please say ..... there seem to be other variables too as the raring updates and upgrade built into the script added the newer kernel ..... think it was the 23 one I ended up on - and there seemed to be some timing problems being reported .... everything seemed to go extremely slowly at one point - possibly the last install before I formatted and started from scratch.

I have 2 working versions still ..... one version is there to completely destroy - so anyone else reading this please note
the installs work ok from DVD ...... its only when I start upgrading using the build script that things mess up.

```

To exit Weston, hit CTRL-ALT-BACKSPACE
 
When using Wayland, to run a program under xwayland, run
xwaylandapp programname --args --args2...
If its a program that supports both Wayland and X
 
You also find Wayland programs under the application launcher in the Wayland Examples section
 
Yesterday was vendredi. Today it is samedi. Tomorrow is dimanche, and lundi 
comes afterwards.
------------------------------------------------------
------------------------------------------------------
In order to be able to run Wayland apps as root, run:
wlsudo programname --args --args2

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-06-01
Passing the 'build' option to test update-rbos is probably the thing I test the *least*. I really only have update-rbos so that in my testing (and in that testing I don't specify the build option, because when I rebuild everything, I run my external build scripts), I can easily get the latest scripts onto my virtualbox test host, and my test laptop for instance if I change the login manager, or just one build script, so I don't have to wait for mksquashfs and everything just for one change to make a new ISO.

Maybe some packages failed to build, but it replaced the installed *rbos packages. This could have happened now that I install all built software as .debs with checkinstall in my scripts...

### Post by 23dornot23d on 2013-06-02
Finally got it to work with 

                                                                                                            Worked with  
$ westoncaller & weston

[IMG]http://i.minus.com/jPcQIL6gYiRYf.jpeg[/IMG]

Now have 2 versions ....... thanks for the help with the Compile_all the command is only going to work in raring
so anyone that followed what was happening there .... will find there are problems upgrading both ways .......
1 .... with rbos-update build ( as that for me stopped Wayland in its tracks )
2 .... with compile-all ( 3 hours - on 8 core processor ) again glibc needs to be at 0.27 .... 

[a]going up one - but then running rbos-update destroys the system as it reverts everything back to raring. 
[b]going up one - grab glibc and drop back to raring ... then other problems occur with the new glibc running ).
[c]never tried compiling glibc .... as memories from earlier led me all over the shop trying to find out how to get rid of
2 versions is almost impossible for me to do with my experience.

Stalled out at this point not getting a successful upgrade where everything works - the original DVD downloads runs very 
well without upgrading though and its the only DVD that I know has a working version of Wayland already running on it.

So ... the system is best loaded from the link on the first page - Great Distro will catch up with this again sometime in the future
to see how its progressing. Thanks for the help along the way too learning some more things and now know where some of the
main directories are for this ..... as well as seeing how it all gets built and its a very long process god only knows how you
write code and then re-compile and re-run to keep testing things out - but best of luck with it.

### Post by 23dornot23d on 2013-06-06
I wanted to follow the road and see where it lead to ........ the thing is with my limited knowledge I soon get stuck
but so far the things we have seen seem to show that its getting some well deserved attention - I doubt we will get away
completely from X this year though .... but if they did there would be tons of work to do to make sure that there are no
glitches in each and every application .... 

I just like seeing what the latest and greatest is ..... seems that if the graphics are refined with all the bells and whistles
it will show Wayland off in a good light ....... enlightenment tests already show that it can be done ..... as well as the
Arch install ..... but at the moment that stand alone ISO maui ....... shows the capabilities to make a nice desktop
but does not yet have the applications and programs running ...... * ( I am sort of guessing here but - without X it may be very limited )

Therefore I would expect to have to stick with X for a brief time while they manage a swap over ..... nothing wrong with running the
apps in X ........ then in the background work on one at a time - making sure they can work seamlessly in wayland.

I am into graphics that is my main reason for having a computer - so if they can improve the graphic experience through speeding it up
and making it more easy to control .... then at some time in the future we should see some massive changes in efficient graphics 
control ....... ( very much like video where they only change the pixels that change on a screen to create good video with small 
data transfer ....... that would be a good step to take ..... ) not sure where the gains are to be made - doing some processing outside of
the main window - do they call it client side working out the differences from the before picture to the required one - then only changing the pixels
that need to be changed on the main screen ...... would it be complex for the computer to do that .... who knows .......

Maybe they are already doing that ......

With the latest version of Enlightenment loaded I am already seeing some nice effects now ..... this to me is the thing that will catch peoples eyes
and as time goes on speed of computers will get better again .... so the graphics like in the old days that pushed the limits of the processor back
then ..... nowadays are handled easily and quickly ...... imagine 5 years from now what sort of graphics we will have.

When I look back to how we started with 16 colours and blocks for graphics ..... the old TI 99 computer my very first one ..... high res graphics it
was said to be capable of at the time ...... ;)

Interesting how things change though - lots of good thought and ideas all being put into practice should give some nice results in the Future
especially if they can improve the efficiency of what data really needs moving from one place to another.

I am waiting now for the x64 version to have a nice installer on Manjaro ...... then I will install that along side the 32 bit version - and then go
for the upgrade to wayland ..... on that ..... as reading all the comments only so much is in 32 bit at this moment in time.

Trying to see where its at ......

[http://www.chaosreigns.com/wiki/Wayland_State](http://www.chaosreigns.com/wiki/Wayland_State)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-07-15
> **cecilpierce said:**
> What is the difference in the two iso's ?
I'm on the reduced iso now from a usb stick, Nice !

The reduced version has all the dev packages removed, and all the symbols removed from the binaries so that the ISO is smaller.

If you're not a developer, there's no difference in the user experience of those CDs, except the ISO is smaller

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-07-15
> **cecilpierce said:**
> OK thanks.
Just did update-user and now get *[ERROR]* failed to set mode on [CRTC:10], worked good before, any fix for that ?

There was no need to run the updater commands. The updater commands only pull the latest scripts and files from my SVN, all the compiled external software is not updated unless you tell it to build.

I'm in the middle of adding session type support to the login manager, where it can handle the describer files as the .desktop file spec. When you ran it, it probably pulled a backup commit.

---

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-07-31
> **cecilpierce said:**
> @nerdopolis

Hows it going, anything new ?

I upgraded the ISO base to Ubuntu Saucy, and I also created a utility wizard (with a zenity frontend) to help users configure devices for supporting weston's multipointer ability

This is an easy way to configure it otherwise, the users would have had to edit udev files manually, and understand udev. 

I don't know if this is good enough for a new ISO. I at least want to get another shell compiling and working on Weston, because my loginmanager has the ability to select a shell before login, like you can on an X login manager. But really only one works...

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-08-11
It's going good. I've been getting some assistance from the Hawaii developer. I now have the Hawaii shell as a selectable option the only thing is that there is something wrong with the way Hawaii is searching for icons. The panel appears blank.

I also added orbital (which I knew about and added BEFORE the Phoronix article :) )
I might also be able to add Consort-Desktop, which is another Weston shell. It's not yet fit for 1.2, but the developer told me he might be able to port it to work on Weston and Wayland 1.2 this week.

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-09-02
No. Just the build scripts are in the SVN, along with my utilities and scripts, such as waylandloginmanager. The build scripts when run, clone the repos, and build them.

You can probably get the new Hawaii commit by running 
```
sudo /usr/bin/Compile/shell
```

but as you are not using FBDEV, this probably isn't affecting you

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-09-04
I think I found out what the issue is, it's related to Weston's new hardware accelerated screen recording:

This Weston commit fixed it
[http://cgit.freedesktop.org/wayland/weston/commit/?id=1f7ce6e3d55ce255187a2a1f07adc778a9108a5f](http://cgit.freedesktop.org/wayland/weston/commit/?id=1f7ce6e3d55ce255187a2a1f07adc778a9108a5f)
however, I decided to give it the packages it needs, so it can enable building the hardware accelerated screen recording into Weston
 [http://rebeccablackos.svn.sourceforge.net/viewvc/rebeccablackos?view=revision&revision=1603](http://rebeccablackos.svn.sourceforge.net/viewvc/rebeccablackos?view=revision&revision=1603)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-09-12
> **cecilpierce said:**
> @nerdopolis

update-rbos kills login, just goes back to login screen, any way I can fix this ?



Again. update-rbos pulls from the *unstable* SVN, and replaces the scripts installed with . I was doing some loginmanager work. 
It's mainly there for my testing, but it was only applicable for use once or twice, but when it was I posted an update to the thread, when I fixed a bug in a launcher file, config file, or runtime (non build script) script, which is when I also released a new ISO IIRC. 


Most of the changes are to the *buildscripts* anyway, which just download the new changes to /usr/bin/Compile/* , or end up depending upon a new build script that I add.
It's not meant to be a real update solution. It does not run the build scripts too. So for instance, I am attempting to add more kde frameworks apps, and mutter, and gnome shell, and I'm working on the build scripts and depends right now. Once I get it to work, I will add appropriate desktop files too in a later commit. If you where to run update-rbos then you will get the new build scripts, and new desktop files, but unless you then know what build scripts to manually run, they will link to things that don't exist, because they haven't been built.



I really appreciate your testing, but unless I say it will actually work, chances are update-rbos will break the system

---

### Post by nerdopolis on 2013-10-24
> **cecilpierce said:**
> @nerdopolis

Any good luck yet ?


No... ...but I guess these features I am trying to add now are alpha and prealpha (mutter-wayland, kdeframeworks and enlightenment wayland)... 

...except for the issues with weston-launch and tty switching... That's a regression. They added a feature in weston to use logind, but it requires logind 207, while saucy only has logind 204. I'm going to try to see if can get logind 207 installed, without b0rking the Franken-Upstart-Systemd hybrid, probably start that tomorrow...

Also since Hawaii is targeting only released versions of Weston, I'm might have to wait until Weston/Wayland 1.4 because there was changes to Weston right after the 1.3 release, and I am currently using Weston master.
(and the breakerage in Hawaii is because it is a special shell plugin, that has special functionality in Weston, not because of any breakerage in the Wayland protocol)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2014-02-09
> **cecilpierce said:**
> Kubuntu installer keeps crashing, tried it on two pc's ?

I wonder if it's related to the weston-desktop-shell hang issues I've been getting on real hardware (I  just noticed the issue yesterday after I pushed the ISO)
[https://bugs.freedesktop.org/show_bug.cgi?id=74752](https://bugs.freedesktop.org/show_bug.cgi?id=74752)

It does not affect VirtualBox/the FB dev backend it seems.

I was able to install it on VirtualBox which uses the Framebuffer. Try starting with a Framebuffer option. (as at least on Intel) Kernel mode setting drivers can provide a virtual /dev/fb0. Using a Framebuffer option in the Grub list when you start the live image, also passes forcefbdev to the kernel command line, which causes WaylandLoginManager to use fbdev.

By the way - this is Gnome - shell running under wayland ....... stilll not getting any browser to work in it yet though ..... time to play.

[IMG]http://i.minus.com/iD3SVoT8o48Q7.jpg[/IMG]



Some great news for Wayland ......... have been searching around to see how far its is coming on and came across this in the mails on KDE ....... so tracked it down
to a blog post .........

[http://blog.martin-graesslin.com/blog/](http://blog.martin-graesslin.com/blog/)

Some things that may help ..... others to help give you some useful information back if that is what you are after ... 
these two small apps included pre-installed

sudo apt-get install htop

sudo apt-get install wireless-tools



would let users know what is running and what is not running .... also may allow users
to fix a small wireless problem ....... as the contact point is showing up just not connecting to
it ........ maybe the atheros wireless ....... but have noticed on the forums - many people are
having problems at this moment in time with wireless ..... might be a coincidence ......

Wayland is now running on my system .... and one thing works so far the battery monitor.

[IMG]http://i.minus.com/ibxe4iqpa8mrlz.jpg[/IMG]

Would post a pic - but as of yet the sd card reader is not being recognized ...... 
brw-rw---- 1 root disk 8, 33 Feb 13 18:12 /dev/sdc1
keith@keith-Aspire-7730ZG:~$ sudo mount /dev/sdc1 /mnt
Appeared to mount in KDE and did not - had to redo it manually ....... odd ..... works now and another tick in a box .....

Photos seem to be the easy way to show what is going on in wayland at the moment - but I keep
having to change systems to read the card and then upload.

Things are getting easier though as one by one each item that evades me is getting fixed.

[I]Still outstanding .

No Sound - on my setup - but as cecil says - its working on his
No Wireless on the second install - but can fix this by going wired
No Hawaii ..... have posted my error log that is all I can do to help here.

The other menu options seem to go in then lock up ....... like orbital - two white round circles appear then it locks[/I]

**Things working now**

Wayland from boot will take me into wayland but only the battery monitor works so far in the top menu
sound works in wayland gnome-shell ....... also the application to show fonts type .......

Wayland Gnome Shell .... goes in and again no applications running as yet but can get to applications screen and choose
( thing is it locks up after choosing something - so far the Qt browser and Firefox browser ....... lock up )

Kde works the best - but the sound does not yet ...... wireless works on one system now - that I set up from wired by adding
wireless tools and network manager for gnome ....... as gnome shell network manager as always picked up on my machine.

Enlightenment works well - but no sound and no wireless in it ....... think it works better
with no composite and no extra graphics enabled from GL .......

Not sure what I can add to this - to let you know how to move forward ........

But adding htop and wireless-tools could help for the next Dvd .

Cheers Cecil ..... you know I do not like IRC can never understand what is going on in them or how

May also try to compile the Alsa drivers into this see if that will make any difference ....... odd as
I have or had sound in the Wayland Gnome-shell ......... I also seem to have sorted all my problems
with wireless out on this machine now for this distro and it is running ever so well.

Once I have sound I can switch completely over to it and start to get more done ...... but at the moment
am hopping between two or 3 distros now ........ not that its much different to any other time when I 
am on here ....... but its annoying when I want to listen to someone who is doing a video on Wayland
and have to jump out of wayland to go somewhere else to actually listen to them.

Are you running ALSA or pulseaudio or OSS on yours now ........ does it say on the mixer what
you are connected through ........

I know the drivers will work for this computer as they are set up in other distros ........ but I have had
this problem before ...... go from one distro or kernel to another one and things change drastically.

Seems to be early on in the boot sequence that something is not happening and that error that I showed
seems to be part of the problem ......... but it still had no sound even before altering the xdg_runtime
so for me - it may be something else that is not quite set up as it should be.

Its knowing how to track it down - and then knowing what action to take that will fix it ..........

Have already tried a re-install of the Kernel ...... the write permission for security reasons .........

Now that looks like its been blocked by another set of programs or write access ........ to the tmp directory

pulseaudio[24080]     [autospawn] core-util.c: Failed to create secure  directory  (/tmp/wayland/pulse): Permission denied

### Post by nerdopolis on 2014-06-09
You can force framebuffer on the installed system by running from a tty

sudo rbos-force-framebuffer

which presents you an easy to use text wizard to add (or remove) 'wlmforcefbdev' to your grub line, to force WaylandLoginManager to use the framebuffer backend for the Wayland servers.


Edit: The username is 'beccaholic' with no password for the tty

---

Edit: The username is 'beccaholic' with no password for the tty

### Post by nerdopolis on 2014-06-17
As far as the flicking goes, that means the selected session is dying fast, and the waylandloginmanager is switching back to not leave the user stranded on an empty tty.

There is no password for the live user session.

I added a feature that shows a 3 second progress bar before it starts launching the session if the login is successful in a new commit today, so there is some verification that the login worked.

Try using a fallback session, I think it's a mesa issue...

Is tty switching not working on of your cards? Some keyboards are pesky and don't send the f* key unless you press function, so verify it isn't that first?

### Post by nerdopolis on 2014-08-18
> **cecilpierce said:**
> Any thing yet ?

Well I do use the enlightenment branch with the Wayland improvements in my build script [https://git.enlightenment.org/core/enlightenment.git/log/?h=devs/devilhorns/e_comp_wl](https://git.enlightenment.org/core/enlightenment.git/log/?h=devs/devilhorns/e_comp_wl) and I also added a workaround because right now it sets the desktop screen size to 1920x1200 without my workaround right now.

Supertux is done porting to SDL2, it works as a native Wayland client which means I finally have an actual SDL2 program on my live cd that isn't just an sdl2 unit test.

Kwin merged their first commits for kwin_wayland today actually, so I'll follow what they do, so I know how to change my wsession file to support starting a kwin wayland session. I already have buildscripts that work for kwin

Wayland 1.6 is due soon, which I will release a new ISO soon after that.

Rebooted,now back to terminal at login showing password but I can get into gnome shell ?

### Post by nerdopolis on 2014-09-10
those unfound module errors in enlightenment are because those modules aren't compatible with enable wayland only compile, and I have to disable them. It seems even if they are disabled from the build, they are still hardcoded and enlightenment tries to load them.

I took you suggestion and increased the font size in Enlightenment

Do you have any logs of the failed session? I assume you're talking about the user session so that would be ~/waylandserver.log or ~/weston.log
If it's the loginmanagerdisplay that's /var/log/loginmanagerdisplay.log

What hardware was this tried on?


I doubt that adding cms-colord.so is the actual fix, as not only is that module getting loaded through the Weston command line option, but that's just to talk to colord...

I am seeing that the first time the loginmanager starts, that it "locks" tty switching sometimes, which will make the starting of the second Weston fail, until the first one is terminated with CTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE

The ISO might not be released for a while, as there are a few issues with some upstream things: orbital now depends on libweston which isn't in Weston yet, Hawaii depends on unmereged changes to qtwayland, and the Calligra suite's QT5 port has been stopped about 9 months ago, and they are going to be resuming it soon, and KDE Plasma does not have Wayland support yet

### Post by nerdopolis on 2014-12-09
> **cecilpierce said:**
> @nerdopolis

Just did update-rbos and it don't boot anymore, tried a few things to get it going but no luck.

Is there a new iso in the works soon ?

There will be a new ISO soon, but as Kwin's wayland support is still not working yet, libweston wasn't merged into Weston for orbital yet, and QWaylandOutput wasn't merged into QtWayland yet for Hawaii yet, and Calligra is soon to start the QT5 port again, I don't have any new features that justifies me pushing ~3GB worth of ISO files onto SourceForge yet...

I just finished making the waylandloginmanager more secure that it doesn't need weston-launch anymore, and instead uses logind. As logind had some fixes after the v212 I had on that ISO, it now requires logind v216 due to some fixes. 

The buildscript that gets called during the ISO build process (on the SVN, and since it is an SVN file, it was replaced when you ran update-rbos). However the script did not get run when update-rbos is run, as update-rbos was mainly there for me to do testing of small changes on a virtual machine without having to rebuild an ISO.

Running update-rbos may break things as it always pulls the latest SVN files, and overwrites them (most of the files listed by dpkg -L rbos-rbos although some might change if I was to remove or add files to the SVN), and some of the changes I make might be incompatible, that requires a new action from the ISO build process, for instance installing a new dependency from the first tier packages (with apt-get), or adding a new build script in /usr/bin/Compile for a new feature, which won't get run by update-rbos, or I might make a change such as in this instance where I required a recompile of one of the packages that gets recompiled by the build scripts.

Also, running update-rbos at the wrong time might be when I accidentally commit a bug in the SVN that prevents something from working, for instance the time when I was removing Xorg from the startup path, and put the path to the new init script for /usr/bin/waylandloginmanager as /usr/bin/waylandlogin**manger** :p and it would not work. Recently I also made a mistake that was preventing weston from starting due to leaving a stray argument in the script by mistake.

However in this instance, you should be able to fix it by running
sudo /usr/bin/Compile/systemd

I think that is the only major change in this instance that is preventing waylandloginmanager from running correctly on an older not rebuilt system...

Most of the times running update-rbos is not going to give you anything new and cooler, as I pretty much created it for my own testing. I think I only had users run it after I realized after I made a small mistake back in 2013 that prevented it from starting up, and it was recent enough that I made only a small change.

---

### Post by nerdopolis on 2014-12-12
Try to remove /etc/systemd/system/display-manager.service
and make sure the line in /etc/X11/default-display-manager says
/usr/bin/waylandloginmanager
It's probably a GDM update that clobbered over waylandloginmanager.

I made a change to workaround this so that GDM won't clobber waylandloginmanager from the startup path for the next ISO. (update-rbos won't fix this as I changed a build file to do so)

### Post by nerdopolis on 2016-08-20
There wasn't many user visibile things that I changed: Orbital is on the ISO for the first time in a while, the wallpapers in some DE's were changed to have the same one in Weston, and the Wayland Login Manager has short descriptions for each DE, just for Orbment, and Sway to tell users the key bindings

### Post by nerdopolis on 2016-10-12
Mostly newer versions of the software that is actually compiles, so not much changed in my scripts.
The ISOs are now built in separate mount and process namespaces which makes the build process a bit cleaner, but most people aren't building ISOs
The WaylandLoginManager now supports showing descriptions for each Desktop Environment. This is so that I could have a good way to tell users how to use Sway and Orbment
I better customized most of the DEs to have the RBOS wallpaper
and I improved the way the checkinstall packages are built, namely in the smaller ISOs so that most files can pass a dpkg verification
